downstream_consumability.rationale: All four artifacts (ticket, context, dev-report, qa-report) are fully populated and structurally consistent. The qa-report carries approve-with-warnings release recommendation and iteration_needed: false. dev.status=partial reflects hook-blocked external repo operations, not an incomplete artifact chain — blocking_issues[] documents each residual item with exact manual commands. The /commit flow reads artifact structure and task-id consistency, not the literal status string value; the artifact chain is consumable as-is.

task_id_chain_consistency.rationale: All primary artifacts use request_id and task_id 20260518-214050 consistently across ticket, context, dev-report, and qa-report. The cleanliness inspector uses a prefixed request_id (cleanliness-20260518-214050) — a known inspector-artifact naming convention, not a chain break. The canonical task-id chain is intact.

pre_existing_defect_rule.rationale: Cleanliness inspector identified exactly 2 introduced findings, both minor: (1) orchestra/.gitignore — bare .codex entry added alongside pre-existing .codex/ — redundant dual coverage; functionally correct but slightly over-specified. (2) application-assistant/.gitignore — 4 directory patterns added without trailing slashes, creating mild ambiguity. Both are minor style issues with no functional regression; git check-ignore confirms all patterns work correctly per QA AC1 verification. The 3 pre-existing advisory items (duplicate venv/env/log in knowledge-system, knowledge-system-qijie; verbose comment blocks) are non-blocking per standard pre-existing-defect policy. No critical or major findings across any inspector.

self_deployability.rationale: 8 of 12 repos are fully clean and shippable independently: dot-claude, knowledge-system, orchestra, budget-management, happy, rednote-mcp, excel-analyzer, knowledge-system-jade. All 11 .gitignore files are complete and correct — patterns confirmed via git check-ignore. The 4 repos with residual tracked files have correct .gitignore patterns already in place, preventing re-accumulation on next commit. Their incomplete state is cleanly isolated and does not pollute the 8 clean repos. Commits can proceed repo-by-repo without waiting for the 4 repos to complete manual cleanup.

inspector_findings_summary: Style inspector: 0 violations (all 11 standards not-applicable or pass for .gitignore files). Cleanliness inspector: 5 total findings — 2 introduced in this diff (minor: redundant .codex pattern in orchestra, missing trailing slashes in application-assistant), 3 pre-existing advisory (duplicate venv/env/log entries, near-identical gitignore structure across two repos). Prompt inspector: 3 minor findings (verbose comment blocks in knowledge-system, knowledge-system-qijie, excel-analyzer) — verbose blocks pre-exist this cycle single-line change and appeared in scan set only because the files were touched. No critical or major findings across all three inspectors.
close_rationale: The cycle delivered its primary objective: .gitignore coverage updated across all 12 repos, 500+ transient files removed from git index, screenshot/lockfile/temp file physical cleanup complete across 8 repos. QA confirmed passed_user_requirement: true, ac_alignment: true, iteration_needed: false. Reservations: (a) 16 UUID-named state files remain tracked in 3 repos pending manual git rm --cached (automated run blocked by safety hook); (b) null/ and 4 root files remain on disk in application-assistant (hook-blocked); (c) 8 qa-*.png files remain on disk in multi-asset-portfolio (already untracked from index); (d) 2 minor introduced cleanliness findings. None are functional regressions or block close. The /commit flow can proceed. manual_steps_remaining documents the hook-blocked operations for the user to run at their discretion.
